data.title: Meta Platforms (META) to Spend $65 Million Pushing 'AI Agenda' During U.S. Midterms
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) is planning to spend $65 million this year to push its "AI agenda" during the U.S. midterm election cycle.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Times (NYT) is reporting that Meta is planning to support state politicians who are friendly to the artificial intelligence (AI) industry, starting with politicians based in Texas and Illinois. This year's political spending is the biggest ever from Meta Platforms, which owns social media sites Facebook and Instagram.

The millions being spent during this year's midterms is a reversal for Meta, which previously made only small donations to political action committees and contributed to presidential inaugurations after elections had occurred. But now, Meta Platforms is getting more politically active as it worries about regulatory threats to the AI sector.

Meta's Super PACs

In addition to spending $65 million to support pro-AI candidates and lobby for supportive legislation, Meta Platforms is also starting two new super PACs. One group, Forge the Future Project, is backing Republicans. Another, Making Our Tomorrow, is getting behind Democratic candidates who are running for seats in the U.S. Congress.

The new Super PACs join two others that Meta has already launched, one focused on California and the other an umbrella organization. Meta's political activities are set to begin in Texas, where the company has three AI data center projects, and where politicians are debating imposing a moratorium on the construction of new data centers.

Is META Stock a Buy?

The stock of Meta Platforms has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 43 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 39 Buy and four Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average META price target of $852.42 implies 34% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: U.S. and Iran Tensions Continue to Rise – Should Investors Buy a Defense ETF?
data.description:
As tensions between the U.S. and Iran continue to rise, investors are paying closer attention to the defense sector. Indeed, recent nuclear talks failed to resolve key disagreements, and U.S. officials have made it clear that military action remains an option if diplomacy breaks down. At the same time, Iran has carried out military exercises near the Strait of Hormuz, while the U.S. has deployed aircraft carriers to the region. In this kind of environment, many investors begin looking for ways to gain exposure to defense spending trends.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

One straightforward way to do that is through defense-focused exchange-traded funds (ETFs). For example, the iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA), the Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA), and the SPDR S&P Aerospace & Defense ETF (XAR) all provide diversified exposure to major aerospace and military contractors. Rather than picking a single stock, these ETFs hold baskets of companies involved in weapons systems, aircraft, missile defense, and other security technologies.

As a result, they allow investors to participate in the broader defense theme while reducing company-specific risk. In addition, each fund has a slightly different focus. For instance, ITA provides broad exposure to large U.S. defense and aerospace companies. Meanwhile, PPA includes firms tied not only to traditional defense but also homeland security. On the other hand, XAR places greater weight on small and mid-sized defense names, which can offer higher growth potential.

Which Defense ETF is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the three defense ETFs mentioned above, analysts think that XAR has the most room to run. In fact, XAR's price target of $315.14 per share implies 12.9% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 2/18/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Wednesday as tech stocks lead the rally.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AI-related stocks are getting a boost after Nvidia (NVDA) announced that Meta Platforms (META) would purchase millions of its GPUs and CPUs as part of an expanded partnership. The energy sector is also a bright spot on the day, with oil stocks trading higher as tensions heighten between the U.S. and Iran.

U.S. Industrial Sector Shows Strength ahead of PCE Inflation Data

Furthermore, traders are also focusing on a strong update from the industrial sector. U.S. industrial production increased by 0.7% in January from the prior month, registering the highest rate of growth since February 2025, according to a new report from the Fed. Factory efficiency is also on the rise, with capacity utilization at 75.6% for the month, the highest since September and increasing from 75.2% in December.

Looking ahead, the Bureau of Economic Analysis will publish December's Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) index on Friday. The BEA will also release core PCE, the Fed's preferred gauge of inflation, which is expected to increase by 2.8% year-over-year.

data.title: YOLO vs. CNBS vs. MSOS: Which Cannabis ETF is Tanking the Hardest in 2026?
data.description:
Cannabis ETFs have struggled since the start of the year. That weakness comes despite Granville's projections that the global legal cannabis market could reach about $216.76 billion by 2033, growing at a compound annual rate of roughly 13.5%.

Funds such as the AdvisorShares Pure Cannabis ETF (YOLO), Amplify Seymour Cannabis ETF (CNBS), and AdvisorShares Pure U.S. Cannabis ETF (MSOS) have seen uneven performance and shifting capital flows in 2026, underscoring cautious investor positioning.

YOLO Logs Outflows as Sell Signals Persist

data.title: Commodity ETFs with the Lowest Expense Ratios – GLD, PPLT
data.description:
We all have to look after our pennies and our cents these days. That's true down at the shops, in the cafe or investing in ETFs.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Cost-savvy investors don't just look at returns or assets under management – they also look at expense ratios.

An ETF's expense ratio shows how much it costs to operate. The expense ratio is typically expressed as a percentage of a fund's average net assets and can include various operational costs and annual fees. The cost is taken out of a fund's return, so it impacts how much an investor can get back in their pockets.

Let's look at two of the lowest expense ratios in our Best Commodity ETFs list – the SPDR Gold Shares ETF (GLD) and the Aberdeen Standard Physical Platinum Shares ETF (PPLT).

GLD

The SPDR Gold Shares ETF offers investors a direct and efficient way to access the gold market. As a cornerstone in the category of Precious Metals, GLD is distinctly focused on gold, allowing investors to tap into the allure and historical significance of this timeless asset. It tracks the LBMA Gold Price PM Index ($/ozt).

Over the last 3 months, its share price is up 23.58%. It has $174.61 billion in assets under management and an expense ratio of 0.40%.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: US Industrial Production Ramps to 11-Month High as Trade Uncertainty Eases
data.description:
U.S. industrial production rose by 0.7% month-over-month in January, marking the highest rate of growth since February 2025, according to a new report from the Fed. Manufacturing activity, which accounts for roughly 75% of industrial production, increased by 0.6%, also the highest since February 2025.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Bringing production back to the U.S. is a key goal of the Trump administration. With trade uncertainty easing and accommodative tax policies, manufacturers, both domestic and foreign, are increasingly investing in U.S. factories.

Industrial Production Gains Momentum

Furthermore, factories are becoming more efficient. During the month, capacity utilization was 75.6%, the highest since September and improving from 75.2% during the previous month. Total industrial utilization was 76.2%, registering a four-month high.

Manufacturing activity could continue to gain. Earlier this month, Institute for Supply Management's manufacturing index for January came in at 52.6, indicating expansion, while manufacturing employment increased for the first time since late 2024.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Time to Rotate Back into Mag 7 Stocks,' Says HSBC
data.description:
Investor fears about big tech and hyperscalers may be overblown, according to Max Kettner, chief multi-asset strategist at HSBC (HSBC). He believes that the recent market selloff has gone too far, especially when you look at how profitable these companies still are. For example, the largest stocks in the S&P 500 (SPY), including Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta (META), Broadcom (AVGO), Tesla (TSLA), and Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B), now generate a combined return on equity of about 65%, compared to just 15% in 2017–2018.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Kettner argues that valuations now look unusually attractive. For instance, Amazon is trading near its lowest price-to-earnings ratio in 20 years. Meanwhile, the "Magnificent Seven" hyperscalers (MAGS) trade at around 26 times earnings, which is very close to the Russell 2000 (IWM) at roughly 25 times, despite the fact that nearly half of the Russell 2000 companies have negative earnings. Because of this mismatch, HSBC believes investors may have overreacted.

As a result, the firm recommends rotating out of high-beta momentum stocks and back into the Mag Seven, while also adding exposure to parts of the economy that could benefit from improving consumer strength. Specifically, HSBC favors homebuilders, regional banks, transportation ETFs, and retail funds. The reasoning is that these sectors could benefit from large tax refunds that are expected this year. Kettner also believes that economists have consistently underestimated the strength of U.S. consumers.

Which Magnificent Seven Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the Magnificent 7 stocks mentioned above, analysts think that Microsoft stock has the most room to run. In fact, Microsoft's average price target of $593.38 per share implies 48% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 'Don't Believe the Hype,' Says Investor as Nvidia Stock Faces a Reality Check
data.description:
The supercharged growth of Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) has been one of the defining market stories of the past few years. Unless you've been living under a rock, you know its GPUs sit at the heart of the AI revolution, powering the vast majority of compute capacity in data centers worldwide.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Its revenue and margins have become the stuff of legend, and the company continues to break sales records quarter after quarter. Many expect its upcoming Q4 FY2026 financial results, due on February 25, to continue this lucrative trend, especially as hyperscalers double down on their AI capex spending in 2026.

Indeed, a huge chunk of this cash will likely find its way into Nvidia's coffers. Yet, with NVDA shares largely moving sideways over the past six months, does this pause present a timely entry point before the next leg higher?

Not according to one investor, known by the pseudonym Oriental Trader, who believes that even after trading sideways, NVDA is simply too expensive. "The market is pricing NVDA to perfection to infinity," emphasizes the investor.

Oriental argues that Nvidia's lofty valuation hinges on one key premise – that hyperscalers will keep pouring massive sums into AI infrastructure year after year. If that spending ever cools, even a little, the whole thesis starts to wobble.

And that's where things get interesting. Oriental points out that the very companies driving that AI buildout – Microsoft, Meta, Amazon, Alphabet, and Oracle – have all pulled back from their all-time highs, suggesting investors may already be questioning how long this spending boom can really last.

The investor argues that the market is punishing the massive capex moves, and predicts that this will cause management to call into question the cost-benefit analysis of continuing to spend at such an astronomical rate.

"Recent stock price declines among major tech companies make a broad AI capex slowdown increasingly likely, threatening NVDA's growth narrative," adds Oriental.

Moreover, despite NVDA's lagging share price of late, the investor posits that it remains elevated with a forward price-to-earnings ratio of close to 40x. Oriental expects that multiple to come down once the expected capex crunch materializes.

And thus, concludes Oriental, the best way to take advantage of the AI revolution is to look beyond Nvidia.

"I see a diversified basket of major tech companies as a more prudent long-term bet than NVDA at current valuations," sums up Oriental, who rates NVDA a Strong Sell. (To watch Oriental Trader's track record, click here)

That's a decidedly contrarian view. On Wall Street, the overwhelming majority remain firmly in Nvidia's corner. With 37 Buys, just 1 Hold, and a single Sell, NVDA carries a Strong Buy consensus rating. Meanwhile, the 12-month average price target of $260.06 implies ~38% upside from current levels. (See NVDA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Boosted by Tech Strength ahead of Fed Minutes
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Wednesday as tech stocks lead the way ahead of the release of January's Fed minutes at 2 p.m. Eastern Time.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Every tech stock in the index is in the green, led by a 2% gain in Nvidia (NVDA). On Tuesday, the semiconductor leader announced that it would expand its partnership with Meta Platforms (META), which includes a commitment for millions of CPUs and Blackwell and Rubin GPUs.

"Through deep codesign across CPUs, GPUs, networking and software, we are bringing the full NVIDIA platform to Meta's researchers and engineers as they build the foundation for the next AI frontier," said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang.

Ahead of the Fed minutes, the odds of a 25 bps interest rate cut at the next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18 stand at a slim 5.9%. By the end of the year, however, traders expect two rate cuts, placing the federal funds rate between 3.00% and 3.25%.

Meanwhile, domestic production is on the rise. In January, U.S. industrial production increased by 0.7% from the prior month, marking the highest rate of growth since February 2025. On top of that, manufacturing activity, which accounts for roughly 75% of industrial production, increased by 0.6%, the highest in 11 months. Domestic production remains a key priority for the Trump administration, and with trade tensions easing and favorable tax policies in place, both domestic and foreign manufacturers are boosting investment in U.S. factories.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.59% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Housing Starts Leap to 5-Month High as Affordability Concerns Persist
data.description:
Homebuilders, like Lennar (LEN) and D.R. Horton (DHI), are back in business. In December, housing starts increased by 6.2%, the highest level in five months, to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 1.4 million homes. Single-family housing starts, which account for the majority of homebuilding, rose by 4.1% to an annualized rate of 981,000 homes.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In another positive sign for the homebuilding industry, building permits, which signal future construction activity, grew by 4.3% to an annualized rate of 1.45 million, registering the highest level since March.

Builder Confidence Slips as Buyers Remain Hesitant

Improving housing market conditions were likely behind the rise in both housing starts and building permits. Homebuilder sentiment ticked higher as 2025 came to a close, although confidence has since slipped as concerns mount over affordability.

"While the majority of builders continue to deploy buyer incentives, including price cuts, many prospective buyers remain on the sidelines," said National Association of Home Builders Chairman Buddy Hughes. Furthermore, buyers must still grapple with mortgage rates above 6%, while builders face elevated construction costs.
